Retrieved evidence:
- source chat:<0> [chat_turn role=USER observedAt=2026-05-08T12:36:03.417394Z]: Memory MVP lesson for the NoMemoryVerifier component: Situation: ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner can incorrectly return need_memory=false for ambiguous recall questions. Action: run one short model-based verification call before accepting the no-memory plan; do not use hard-coded substring heuristics. Result: we avoid brittle lexical hacks while still catching recall intent. Lesson: for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching.

structured.fullText: For ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, NoMemoryVerifier should run a cheap short model-based verification step instead of relying on substring heuristics.
